preamble: The Voice Loss of the initial alveolar sibilant [z] to [s] is a major catalyst for tonal Register Split. Voiced sibilants like [z] produced a lower Pitch Contour on the following vowel (Low Register), while voiceless [s] produced a higher pitch (High Register). When [z] devoiced, the pitch difference was phonemicized as distinct tones.
phoneticEffects: Voice Loss, Devoicing, Register Split, Tonogenesis

examples.note: Initial voicing in sibilants in Mon-Khmer provided the phonetic environment for the development of the low-register tones in Vietnamese.
Early Middle Chinese voiced sibilants (Quánzhuó) devoiced, phonemicizing the low-register pitch contours in the resulting tonal system.
Proto-Tai initial *z is reconstructed as a source for the low-register tonal contours during the Tai-Kadai Register Split.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Pittayaporn, P. (2009). The Phonology of Proto-Tai.
